subject: english
text: I. Answer the following questions in 2 or 3 sentences. 1) Mercy is compared to gentle from heaven. How is this comparison apt? 2) The speaker compares the power of the king with the power of mercy, saying that mercy is mightiest of mightiest. How does she justify this? 3) The speaker says that mercy is twice blessed? What does he mean by this? OR How do you interpret the poet’s interpretation of mercy as twice blessed? 4) Sceptre shows the force of temporal power. In contrast what or whose quality does mercy stand for? 5) The poem quality of mercy has fourteen lines. But it is not a sonnet. Justify the statement.
group_heading: Answer in Two or More Sentences

subject: english
text:
“It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven
Upon the place beneath, it is twice blest”
A) What is compared to gentle rain from heaven?
B) What blesses twice?
C) Why does the speaker feel it blesses twice ?

subject: english
text:
His sceptre shows the force of temporal power,
The attribute to awe and majesty,
A) What is Sceptre?
B) How does the sceptre show the temporal poet?
C) In contrast, what quality does mercy stand for?

subject: english
text:
“’Tis mightiest in the mightiest”
A) There are two ‘mightiest’ things . What do they stand for?
B) What does the word ‘mightiest’ mean?
C) What does the speaker intend to say by comparing two mighty things, here?

subject: english
text:
It blesseth him that gives, and him that takes
A) What does ‘it’ refer to, here?
B) How does it bless the one who gives?
C) How does it bless the one who takes?

subject: english
text:
Mercy is above the sceptred sway.
A) What is a sceptre?
B) How is mercy above the sceptred sway?
C) Which is above the sceptre sway?

subject: english
text: Write the summary of the poem “Quality of Mercy”

subject: english
text: IV. 12. Identify the figure of speech in the following lines of  the poem: i) “It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven” ii) “It blesseth him that gives and him that takes” iii) “And earthly power doth then show likest God’s” 24
